upstream.test: should report the failed function calls to symbols

entries.reason: Calling an undefined symbol rejects with Nunjitsu's structured render error; exact Nunjucks diagnostic text is outside the compatibility contract.

upstream.test: should report the failed function calls to lookups

entries.reason: Calling an undefined bracket lookup rejects semantically; exact rendered function paths in diagnostic text are outside the compatibility contract.

upstream.test: should report the failed function calls to calls

entries.reason: Calling an undefined dotted lookup rejects semantically; exact rendered function paths in diagnostic text are outside the compatibility contract.

upstream.test: should report full function name in error

entries.reason: Long undefined lookup calls reject without truncation affecting evaluation; Nunjitsu does not claim compatibility for exact diagnostic formatting.

upstream.test: should report the failed function calls w/multiple args

entries.reason: Undefined dotted and bracket calls reject with multiple arguments as they do with one; exact diagnostic paths are outside the compatibility contract.

upstream.test: should allow for undefined macro arguments in the last position

entries.reason: Inline macros accept an undefined final argument and render it as an empty value.

upstream.test: should allow for objects without a prototype macro arguments in the last position

entries.reason: The runtime suite passes the same null-prototype record as the final macro argument through Nunjitsu's copied safe-value boundary and verifies its property output.

upstream.test: should not read variables property from Object.prototype

entries.reason: Nunjitsu deliberately copies own data properties and ignores inherited Object.prototype values rather than rendering their source text.

upstream.test: should parse basic types

entries.reason: Asserts the public Nunjucks parser AST object model, which Nunjitsu intentionally does not expose.

upstream.test: should parse aggregate types

entries.reason: Observable parenthesized group, array, and dictionary behavior is verified through rendered output because Nunjitsu exposes no parser AST API.

upstream.test: should parse variables

entries.reason: Static text surrounding a symbol interpolation is verified through rendered output because Nunjitsu exposes no parser AST API.

upstream.test: should parse operators

entries.reason: Requires a Nunjucks parser extension or host-defined tag, which the direct-string API does not expose.

upstream.test: should parse tilde

entries.reason: Tilde concatenation is verified through rendered output rather than a Nunjucks Concat node.

upstream.test: should parse operators with correct precedence

entries.reason: Boolean, comparison, membership, test, and observable not grouping are exercised through rendered results rather than AST nesting.

upstream.test: should parse blocks

entries.reason: Standalone inline block bodies and matching named closing tags are verified without template inheritance.

upstream.test: should accept attributes and methods of static arrays, objects and primitives

entries.reason: Nunjitsu deliberately does not expose JavaScript prototype methods to templates; equivalent safe operations use built-in filters under the documented copied-value security model.

upstream.test: should parse include tags

entries.reason: Requires named-template loading, includes, imports, or inheritance, which the secure direct-string API does not support.

upstream.test: should parse for loops

entries.reason: Flat loop targets and container-sensitive iteration are verified through rendered output rather than exposed parser nodes.

upstream.test: should parse for loops with else

entries.reason: Loop else behavior is verified through rendered output with fixed autoescape false.

upstream.test: should parse filters

entries.reason: Chained and argument-bearing filters are exercised through the built-in filter corpus rather than Filter AST nodes.

upstream.test: should parse macro definitions

entries.reason: Macro parameters, defaults, bodies, and calls are verified through rendered behavior rather than Macro AST nodes.

upstream.test: should parse call blocks

entries.reason: Caller-body behavior is verified through rendered call blocks and the dedicated closed CallBlock AST node rather than upstream AST shapes.

upstream.test: should parse call blocks with args

entries.reason: Call-block bindings plus positional and keyword arguments are verified through rendered output.

upstream.test: should parse raw

entries.reason: Raw scanning and argument-free opening-tag validation are verified through inline rendered output with fixed autoescape false.

entries.reason: Broken variable syntax is verified as inert raw text with fixed autoescape false.

upstream.test: should parse raw with broken blocks

entries.reason: Broken block syntax is verified as inert raw text with fixed autoescape false.

upstream.test: should keep track of whitespace

entries.reason: Individual whitespace tokens are part of the replaced Nunjucks lexer API; observable template whitespace is covered by render assertions.

upstream.test: should parse basic types

upstream.test: should parse function calls

upstream.test: should parse groups

upstream.test: should parse arrays

upstream.test: should parse filters

upstream.test: should parse operators

entries.reason: Operator tokens are verified through evaluated output, while repeated identical unary signs are rejected according to the pinned compiler's observable output.

upstream.test: should parse comments

entries.reason: Opaque comment termination and applicable observable language behavior are exercised through the synchronous direct-string API.

entries.reason: Exact upstream exception formatting and source locations are outside the documented compatibility contract.

upstream.test: should include error line when exception raised in user function

entries.reason: The runtime capability test verifies trusted host callback failures propagate, while exact upstream line formatting and live context functions are outside the contract.

entries.reason: Requires named-template loading, includes, imports, or inheritance, which the secure direct-string API does not support.

entries.reason: Requires named-template loading, includes, imports, or inheritance, which the secure direct-string API does not support.

entries.reason: All copied-value sort assertions are ported. Exact throwOnUndefined diagnostics are outside the documented exception-format contract.

upstream.test: groupby

entries.reason: All copied-value grouping assertions, including nested and undefined attributes, are ported. Exact throwOnUndefined diagnostics are outside the documented exception-format contract.

upstream.test: should return length of a list literal
should output 0 for a missing context variable
should output string length for string variables
should output string length for a SafeString variable
should output the correct length of a string created with new String()

entries.reason: Nunjitsu accepts primitive strings and SafeString values; exotic JavaScript wrapper objects are rejected at the copied-value boundary.

upstream.test: should output 0 for a literal "undefined"
should output 0 for a literal "null"
should output 0 for an Object with no properties
should output 1 for an Object with 1 property
should output the number of properties for a plain Object, not the value of its length property
should output the length of an array
should output the full length of a sparse array
should output the length of an array created with "new Array"
should output the length of an array created with "new Array" with user-defined properties

entries.reason: Nunjitsu rejects arrays with custom properties at the copied-value boundary rather than exposing ambient JavaScript object state to the interpreter.

callable should detect callability

entries.reason: Callable detection is ported for sealed template callables, while live context functions and callable use by non-identity tests are rejected by the closed authority contract.

upstream.test: defined should detect definedness
should support "is defined" in {% if %} expressions
should support "is not defined" in {% if %} expressions
undefined should detect undefinedness
none/null should detect strictly null values
divisibleby should detect divisibility
escaped should test whether or not something is escaped
even should detect whether or not a number is even
odd should detect whether or not a number is odd
mapping should detect Maps or hashes

entries.reason: Plain copied records are ported; JavaScript Map instances are outside the TemplateValue contract.

upstream.test: falsy should detect whether or not a value is falsy
truthy should detect whether or not a value is truthy

ge should detect whether or not a value is greater than or equal to another
lessthan than should detect whether or not a value is less than another

ne should detect whether or not a value is not equal to another

upstream.test: number should detect whether a value is numeric
string should detect whether a value is a string
equalto should detect value equality
sameas should alias to equalto
lower should detect whether or not a string is lowercased
upper should detect whether or not a string is uppercased
should have range
should have cycler

entries.reason: Primitive cycler behavior is ported; retaining sealed callable authority is an intentional security deviation outside the upstream assertions.

upstream.test: should have joiner

entries.reason: Primitive joiner behavior is ported; retaining sealed callable authority or discarding method arguments is intentionally rejected outside the upstream assertions.

upstream.test: should allow addition of globals

entries.reason: Callable globals are configured immutably and invoked positionally; keyword syntax and sealed callable arguments are intentional security deviations.

upstream.test: should allow chaining of globals

entries.reason: Nunjitsu accepts multiple callable globals in one immutable engine configuration rather than exposing a chainable mutator.

upstream.test: should allow getting of globals

entries.reason: Nunjitsu does not expose Nunjucks Environment instances or a mutable global getter API.

upstream.test: should allow getting boolean globals

entries.reason: Nunjitsu exposes callable engine globals through immutable capabilities; copied boolean values belong in render context.

upstream.test: should fail on getting non-existent global

entries.reason: Nunjitsu has no public global getter; invoking an unregistered template global fails through the render API.

upstream.test: should pass context as this to global functions

entries.reason: The new capability API does not emulate Nunjucks this.lookup; global arguments are copied explicitly and callbacks receive a render-owned AbortSignal.

upstream.test: should be exclusive to each environment

entries.reason: The runtime test verifies immutable global registries are isolated between independently created engines.

upstream.test: should return errors from globals

entries.reason: Applicable observable language behavior is exercised through the synchronous direct-string API.
